item.title: 访问日志中各个站点的总数据传输 | Total data transfer of Individual sites from access log
item.description:
一旦暂停，ajinm 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，ajinm 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，所有由 ajinm 发布的帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ajinm 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 ajinm 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 ajinm 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Once suspended, ajinm will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ajinm will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ajinm will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ajinm is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ajinm:

ajinm consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ajinm will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: ajinm

item.title: 如何为喜欢减少运动的用户禁用 Webflow 动画 | How to disable Webflow animations for users who prefer reduced motion
item.description:
但是，您的一些访问者可能会对 Webflow 允许您创建的所有褪色、扭曲、移动和缩放感到头晕目眩。 其他人可能只是更喜欢网站更加静态和以信息为中心。

这些用户通常在他们的计算机上打开“减少运动”设置。

尊重这种用户偏好对于提高网站的可访问性很重要，尤其是对于动画较多的网站。

在本文中，我们将向您展示如何在启用首选减少运动时禁用 Webflow 动画和交互。

编码

让我们直接跳到您最可能感兴趣的部分：代码！

这是您可以通过 Webflow 中的站点设置 > 自定义代码选项卡添加到站点页脚的简短脚本：

 <script> // Cancel Webflow animations for users who prefer reduced motion if ( window . matchMedia ( " (prefers-reduced-motion) " ). matches ) { const cancelAnimationsInterval = setInterval ( function () { if ( typeof Webflow == " undefined " || typeof Webflow . require == " undefined " ) { return ; } clearInterval ( cancelAnimationsInterval ); // Stop Webflow's handling of animations Webflow . require ( " ix2 " ). destroy (); // Reset the inline styles for every element that has already been initialized const animatedElementsSelector = " [style*='will-change'], [style*='opacity: 0'] " ; for ( const animatedEl of document . querySelectorAll ( animatedElementsSelector )) { animatedEl . style = "" ; } }, 10 ); } </script>

您应该将此脚本放在页脚自定义代码的最顶部。 否则，它之前的缓慢或繁重的脚本可能会阻止它足够早地停止动画，这可能会导致打开动作减少的访问者出现奇怪的动画内容闪烁。

这个怎么运作

当您在站点的页脚中添加此脚本时，它会出现在结束</body>标记之前。

此时，Webflow 的交互脚本应该已经被加载并初始化，但是没有足够的时间让浏览器渲染动画元素的起始样式。

该脚本首先检查用户是否更喜欢减少动作。 如果他们这样做，它将：

  1. 等到 Webflow 的脚本加载完毕。
  2. 告诉 Webflow 停止处理和处理交互和动画。
  3. 删除 Webflow 已经开始制作动画的元素上的内联样式。

实际上，所有这些都发生在几毫秒内。

从访问者的角度来看，您的网站在加载页面或滚动时看起来就像没有任何动画一样。

对于没有打开“喜欢减少运动”设置的访问者，脚本不会做任何事情：您的网站将按照您的设计出现，动画等等！

之前和之后

这是 Koalati 网站的预览，它使用了这个脚本，无论是否打开了 prefers -reduced-motion设置。

关闭偏好减少运动。

打开偏好减少运动。

要记住的几件事

悬停、聚焦和主动效果不受影响。

此脚本仅禁用通过 Webflow 的交互选项卡生成的动画。

您的悬停、焦点、焦点可见和活动样式不受此脚本的影响，因此将保持不变。

这是设计使然，因为悬停、焦点和活动状态对于您网站的可访问性很重要。

自定义代码生成的动画不受影响。

如果您使用自定义代码（Javascript 和/或 CSS）来创建动画和交互，则此脚本不会禁用这些。

此脚本仅禁用通过 Webflow 的交互选项卡生成的动画和交互。

所有内联样式都从动画元素中删除。

此脚本将自动从通常具有动画效果的元素中删除所有内联样式（当访问者打开了喜欢减少运动时）。

对于大多数 Webflow 站点来说，这不是问题。

但是，如果您有意在某些也具有 Webflow 交互的元素上添加内联样式，则应确保在引入此脚本时这些元素不会被破坏。

如果是，您可以尝试更新animatedElementsSelector以忽略这些元素。

如果这不起作用，您可能需要调整代码以满足您的需求。

如果您将 Webflow Interactions 用于重要组件（例如：菜单汉堡按钮），则此脚本不适合您。

看到脚本禁用所有 Webflow 交互，如果您将交互用于重要组件（例如菜单切换按钮），您将希望实现自己的解决方案。

但是，您应该知道，对重要的交互式组件使用 Webflow 交互通常对于可访问性来说并不是很好，除非您手动添加自定义代码来处理它的可访问性方面。

概括

为喜欢减少运动的用户禁用 Webflow 交互和动画是提高网站可访问性的一种简单方法，尤其是在您使用大量动画的情况下。

幸运的是，对于大多数网站来说，由于我们在本文中提供的简短代码片段，它可以在几分钟内完成。



原文:

Webflow animations and interactions can really make a site shine.

However, some of your visitors might be dizzied by all of the fading, twisting, shifting and scaling that Webflow allows you to create. Others might just prefer websites to be more static and information-focused.

These users often have the "Reduce motion" setting turned on on their computer.

Respecting this user preference is important to improve your site's accessibility, especially for animation-heavy sites.

In this article, we'll show you how you can disable your Webflow animations and interactions when prefers-reduced-motion is turned on.

‍

The code

Let's skip straight to the part you're most likely interested in: the code!

Here is the short script that you can add to your site's footer via the Site settings > Custom code tab in Webflow:

<script> // Cancel Webflow animations for users who prefer reduced motion if (window.matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion)").matches) { const cancelAnimationsInterval = setInterval(function () { if (typeof Webflow == "undefined" || typeof Webflow.require == "undefined") { return; } clearInterval(cancelAnimationsInterval); // Stop Webflow's handling of animations Webflow.require("ix2").destroy(); // Reset the inline styles for every element that has already been initialized const animatedElementsSelector = "[style*='will-change'], [style*='opacity: 0']"; for (const animatedEl of document.querySelectorAll(animatedElementsSelector)) { animatedEl.style = ""; } }, 10); }
</script>

You should place this script at the very top of your footer's custom code. Otherwise, slow or heavy scripts that precede it might prevent it from stopping the animations early enough, which could result in a weird flash of animated content for visitors with reduced motion turned on.

‍

How it works

When you add this script in your site's footer, it appears before the closing </body> tag.

At that point, Webflow's interactions script should already have been loaded and initialized, but not enough time has passed for the browser to render the animated element's starting styles.

The script first checks if the user prefers reduced motions. If they do, it will:

  1. Wait until Webflow's script has loaded.
  2. Tell Webflow to stop processing and handling interactions and animations.
  3. Remove inline styles on elements that Webflow already started animating.

In practice, all of this happens within a few milliseconds.

From the visitor's point of view, your site will simply appear as though it does not have any animations when loading the page or scrolling.

For visitors who don't have the "prefers reduced motion" setting turned on, the script doesn't do anything: your site will appear as you designed it, animations and all!

‍

Before & After

Here's a preview of Koalati's website, which is using this script, with and without the prefers-reduced-motion setting turned on.

‍


With prefers-reduced-motion turned OFF.

‍

With prefers-reduced-motion turned ON.

‍

A few things to keep in mind

Hover, focus and active effects aren't affected.

This script only disables the animations generated via Webflow's Interactions tab.

Your hover, focus, focus-visible and active styles are not affected by this script, and will therefore remain unchanged.

This is by design, as hover, focus and active states are important for your site's accessibility.

‍

Animations generated by custom code aren't affected.

If you use custom code (Javascript and/or CSS) to create animations and interactions, these won't be disabled by this script.

Only animations and interactions generated via Webflow's interactions tab are disabled by this script.

‍

All inline styles are removed from animated elements.

This script will automatically remove all inline styles from the elements that are usually animated (when visitors have prefers-reduced-motion turned on).

For most Webflow sites, this won't be an issue.

However, if you intentionally add inline styles on some elements that also have Webflow interactions, you should make sure that these elements aren't broken when this script is introduced.

If they are, you can try to update the animatedElementsSelector to ignore those elements.

If that doesn't work, you'll likely need to adapt the code to match your needs.

‍

If you use Webflow Interactions for important components (ex.: menu burger button), this script is not adequate for you.

Seeing as the script disables all Webflow interactions, you'll want to implement your own solution if you use Interactions for important components such as a menu toggle button.

However, you should be aware that using Webflow interactions for important interactive components is generally not great for accessibility to begin with, unless you manually add custom code to handle the accessibility side of it.

Summary

Disabling Webflow interactions and animations for users who prefer reduced motion is an easy way to improve your site's accessibility, especially if you use a lot of animations.

Luckily, for most sites, it can be done in just a few minutes thanks to the short code snippet we provide in this article.
item.author: emileperron

item.title: 网络安全工程方法的 4 个好处 | 4 benefits of an engineering approach to cybersecurity
item.description:
经常听到人们谈论对网络安全采取“工程方法”。 但这实际上意味着什么？ 它与传统的网络安全模型有何不同？ 对企业有什么好处？

网络安全工程方法的根源

了解网络安全工程方法的最佳方法是查看它的来源。 从广义上讲，我们可以说它源于网络安全和一般技术领域的三个主要发展：

企业和开发人员现在意识到，在软件开发生命周期的每个阶段都需要考虑安全性。 这导致了技术的哲学转变：越来越多的安全性被视为一个过程，而不是一个特征。

基础设施即服务的兴起：AWS 和其他 IaaS 提供商的成功表明，可以简单地为构建者提供完成工作所需的工具，而不是试图提供现成的解决方案来适应每个用例. 自助服务、可扩展和 API 优先的技术方法的好处是显而易见的（并且经过验证）。

成熟的网络安全行业：网络安全已经成长起来。 今天的行业由拥有数十年经验的人领导。 可用的工具功能强大且多样化。 市场上有一个新的人才、技能和专业知识水平。 正因为如此，今天的安全团队能够通过测试和迭代来改善组织的网络安全状况，并在需要时构建自己的定制工具。

定义的工程方法

综合上述因素，网络安全的工程方法可以定义为使用软件工程的工具、技术和方法实现的网络安全。

具体来说，这意味着：

  • 强调测试和验证以确保可证明的安全性

  • 对组织的安全状况（即安全作为一个过程）进行频繁、迭代的增强的实践

  • 使用版本控制工具来实现快速更改以及在需要时回滚更改的能力

  • 在可用的情况下使用开源工具和公共云基础设施

  • 偏好可根据需要扩大或缩小规模的工具和基础设施

  • 在有意义的情况下构建自定义工具，而不是依赖现成的解决方案

  • 尽可能使用自动化来处理配置和维护

  • 偏爱提供透明度和控制力的供应商，以及具有支持敏捷性和快速部署的销售文化的供应商

对企业的好处

这种网络安全方法为企业提供了许多商业利益：对一般公司和网络安全公司都是如此。 理解这些好处的最好方法是通过对比。 让我们看一下作为工程的网络安全与传统安全模型之间的四个主要区别：

网络安全的工程方法有助于确保可证明的安全性。 使用的工具对使用它们的安全团队是透明的。 对持续测试和增强的关注意味着组织的安全状况不是假设的，而是经过客观验证的。

这与许多大型供应商处理网络安全的方式形成鲜明对比：即通过黑盒解决方案和“相信我们，您是安全的”的态度。

好处：公司知道他们的保障范围——而不是让供应商信守承诺。 这有助于企业更有效地估计和管理风险，这在具有严格合规和责任要求的行业中尤为重要。

网络安全工程中使用的工具和方法支持安全的迭代方法：安全作为一个过程。 这是因为从字面上看，它们是围绕着这样一个假设设计的，即变化是不可避免的，而且很可能会很频繁。

另一方面，传统方法倾向于将安全性视为一种功能或商品。 这种想法的最终结果往往看起来有点像试图建造一堵完美的墙，希望它能坚持下去。 许多大型供应商通过要求客户在对部署进行更改之前获得他们的帮助来强化这种模式。

好处：在当今不断变化的威胁环境中，敏捷性对于良好的安全性至关重要。 但除了网络安全利益之外，还有一些重要的商业利益。 一方面，为变革而构建的工具有助于企业在未来应对不可预见的挑战：新出现的网络安全挑战、新的监管要求，或者仅仅是由高管决定的路线变化。 此外，这些工具允许公司更好地利用他们的技术人员——例如，通过实施先进的安全学科，如检测工程。 对于某些用例，例如DFIR ，使用敏捷、工程优先的工具也带来了直接的竞争优势：即更快的响应时间和提供有吸引力的服务级别协议的能力。

网络安全的工程方法是针对建设者的。 这就是为什么我们经常将 LimaCharlie 比作一个为网络安全专业人员准备的装满乐高积木的盒子：我们为您提供工具、功能和基础设施……您可以构建任何您想要的东西！

不用说，传统供应商不会以这种方式工作。 他们的工具是，嗯，他们是什么。 它们可以在一定程度上进行修改——但通常需要一定程度的供应商干预，而且几乎总是要付出代价。

好处：需要高度定制化解决方案的企业不再受其供应商的摆布。 他们可以简单地构建它们：不需要外部帮助或销售人员的许可。 在一个成熟的网络安全行业，这越来越可行，因为安全团队拥有从事此类工作的专业知识和愿望。

网络安全的工程方法以其供应商的开放性而著称。 原因很明显：供应商不与他们的最终用户竞争——他们将灵活性视为他们的关键价值主张之一。 如上所述，这会提高技术本身的透明度。 但它也体现在实际的方式上：可扩展、自助式使用； 清晰、可预测的定价； 和基于使用的计费。

这与大多数大型网络安全供应商的运作方式几乎相反。 他们的商业模式迫使他们推动供应商锁定，以及所有这一切：长期的最低合同、强制性的产能规划、不透明的定价，以及有时感觉更像是试镜而不是对话的销售过程。

好处：与开放、灵活的供应商合作会带来许多商业利益。 使用传统供应商成本高昂的项目（例如，“淘汰和替换”操作）突然变得可行。 工程优先的供应商通常乐于支持开源工具的集成，从而以更低的成本为公司提供更丰富的安全性。

在 LimaCharlie，我们正在努力让每个人都可以使用网络安全的工程方法。 我们提供免费套餐，让用户可以即时访问我们强大的网络安全功能套件（超过 100 多种，并且还在不断增加）——无需签订合同或致电销售。

我们为Atomic Red Team等工具提供集成，以帮助网络安全工程师开发可测试、可证明的安全性。 我们还提供对许多开源技术的支持，例如Velociraptor和YARA以及Sigma规则。

想了解更多？ 立即尝试 LimaCharlie或预订演示！



原文:

It’s common to hear people talk about taking “an engineering approach” to cybersecurity. But what does this actually mean? How does it differ from the legacy model of cybersecurity? And what are the benefits to the enterprise?

The roots of the engineering approach to cybersecurity

The best way to understand the engineering approach to cybersecurity is to look at where it comes from. Broadly speaking, we can say that it stems from three major developments—both within cybersecurity and also in tech generally:

The growth of DevSecOps : The days of “security as an afterthought” are ending. Enterprises and developers now realize that security needs to be considered in every stage of the software development lifecycle. And this has caused a philosophical shift in tech: increasingly, security is seen as a process rather than a feature.

The rise of infrastructure-as-a-service : The success of AWS and other IaaS providers shows that it’s possible to simply give builders the tools they need to do their work, rather than trying to offer ready-made solutions to fit every use case. The benefits of a self-service, scalable, and API-first approach to technology are clear (and proven).

A maturing cybersecurity industry : Cybersecurity has grown up. The industry today is led by people with decades’ worth of experience. The available tooling is powerful and diverse. There is a new level of talent, skill, and specialized knowledge in the market. Because of this, today’s security teams are capable of improving an organization’s cybersecurity posture through testing and iteration—and of building their own, custom tools when needed.

The engineering approach defined

Putting the above factors together, the engineering approach to cybersecurity can be defined as cybersecurity accomplished using the tools, techniques, and methodologies of software engineering.

Specifically, this means:

  • An emphasis on testing and validation to ensure provable security

  • The practice of making frequent, iterative enhancements to an organization’s security posture (i.e., security as a process)

  • Using version control tools to enable rapid changes and the ability to roll back changes if needed

  • The use of open-source tools and public cloud infrastructure where available

  • A preference for tools and infrastructure that can be scaled up or down based on need

  • Building custom tools rather than relying on off-the-shelf solutions when it makes sense to do so

  • The use of automation to handle provisioning and maintenance when possible

  • A preference for vendors that offer transparency and control—and ones with a sales culture that supports agility and rapid deployment

Benefits to the enterprise

This approach to cybersecurity offers a number of business benefits to the enterprise: both to companies in general and also for cybersecurity firms. The best way to understand these benefits is by way of contrast. Let’s look at four major differences between cybersecurity as engineering and the legacy model of security:

1. Proof vs. Trust

An engineering approach to cybersecurity helps to ensure provable security. The tools used are transparent to the security teams using them. And the focus on continuous testing and enhancement means that an organization’s security posture is not assumed—it’s objectively validated.

This is a stark contrast to the way that many large vendors approach cybersecurity: namely, via black-box solutions and an attitude of “just trust us, you’re safe.”

Benefits : Companies know what they’re covered against—rather than having to take vendors at their word. This helps enterprises estimate and manage risk more effectively, something that is especially important in industries with strict compliance and liability requirements.

2. Iterable vs. Static

The tools and methodologies used in cybersecurity engineering enable an iterative approach to security: security as a process. This is because they are, quite literally, designed around the assumption that change is inevitable, and will likely be frequent.

Legacy approaches, on the other hand, tend to treat security as a feature, or a commodity. The end result of this kind of thinking often looks a bit like an attempt to build a perfect wall in the hope that it will hold. Many large vendors reinforce this model by requiring customers to get their help before making changes to a deployment.

Benefits : In today’s constantly shifting threat landscape, agility is essential to good security. But beyond the cybersecurity benefits, there are some important business benefits as well. For one thing, tools built for change help to future-proof a business against the unforeseen: emerging cybersecurity challenges, new regulatory requirements, or just a change in course dictated by the C-suite. In addition, these tools allow firms to make better use of their skilled personnel—for example, by implementing advanced security disciplines such as detection engineering. For some use cases, such as DFIR, working with agile, engineering-first tools brings direct competitive advantages as well: namely, faster response times and the ability to offer attractive service-level agreements.

3. Customizable vs. One-size-fits-all

The engineering approach to cybersecurity is aimed at builders. This is why we often compare LimaCharlie to a box full of Lego blocks for cybersecurity professionals: We give you the tools, capabilities, and infrastructure…and you build whatever you want!

Legacy vendors, needless to say, don’t work this way. Their tools are, well, what they are. They can be modified to a certain extent—but usually with a cumbersome degree of vendor intervention, and almost always at a cost.

Benefits : Businesses that need highly customized solutions are no longer at the mercy of their vendors. They can simply build them: no outside help or permission from a salesperson required. In a maturing cybersecurity industry, that is increasingly feasible, since security teams have the know-how—and the desire—to do this kind of work.

4. Openness vs Protectionism

The engineering approach to cybersecurity is distinguished by the openness of its vendors. The reason for this is obvious: The vendors aren’t competing with their end users—and they see flexibility as one of their key value propositions. This results in greater transparency around the technology itself, as mentioned above. But it’s also reflected in practical ways: scalable, self-service usage; clear, predictable pricing; and usage-based billing.

This is pretty much the opposite of how most large cybersecurity vendors operate. Their business model forces them to push for vendor lock-in, and all that that entails: long minimum contracts, mandatory capacity planning, opaque pricing, and a sales process that sometimes feels more like an audition than a conversation.

Benefits : Working with open, flexible vendors brings a number of business benefits. Projects that would have been cost-prohibitive using traditional vendors—for example, “rip and replace” operations—suddenly become doable. The inherent flexibility of the IaaS model means fewer contract negotiations as well, reducing overhead for executive and finance teams. And engineering-first vendors are often happy to support integrations for open-source tooling, offering companies richer security at lower costs.

LimaCharlie for cybersecurity engineering

At LimaCharlie, we’re working to make the engineering approach to cybersecurity available to everyone. We offer a free tier that gives users instant access to our suite of powerful cybersecurity capabilities (over 100+ and counting)—no contracts or sales calls required.

We offer integrations for tools like Atomic Red Team in order to help cybersecurity engineers develop testable, provable security. We also offer support for many open-source technologies, such as Velociraptor and YARA and Sigma rules.

Want to learn more? Try LimaCharlie or book a demo today!
item.author: charltonlc

item.title: 网络安全专家访谈：惠特尼冠军 | Cybersecurity Expert Interview: Whitney Champion
item.description:
请介绍您自己并告诉我们您的工作。

我的名字是 Whitney Champion，我是首席架构师，也是Recon InfoSec的联合创始人之一。 基本上，我负责构建和维护我们的安全堆栈、我们的应用程序以及我们的培训平台：网络防御靶场或 NDR。 我们是一家托管安全服务提供商，总部位于德克萨斯州奥斯汀。 我们大约有 15 人，我们提供托管检测和响应服务以及培训。

很酷。 是什么让你想创办一家 MDR 公司？

好吧，在我加入他之前， Eric (Capuano)实际上已经独自经营 Recon 几年了。 这对他来说是一种副业。 他也从事安全行业很长时间。 最初，我和他一起努力让我们的一个副项目OpenSOC启动并运行。 我和他一起帮助实现自动化并将其变为现实，因为他一直在自己做这件事，我有周期可以参与。所以在我们一起工作了一段时间后，我开始为 Recon 工作旁边。 我仍然全职从事我的日常工作，与红帽合作，我真的很怀念创业生活。 在我加入红帽之前，我在一家员工数约为 40 人的公司工作，后来我们发展到 400 人左右。我跳槽到红帽，如你所知，那是一家相当大的公司. 我真的很怀念在狭小的环境中，我怀念磨砺，从头开始构建事物。

我喜欢我们在 Recon 所做的事情，并且知道我们可以对这个行业产生影响，Eric 也是如此。 我们需要全职的基础设施工作和安全工程。 所以，最终，当我们有能力的时候，我全职去了 Recon，几年后，我们来到了这里。

这是一段疯狂的旅程，但我真的为我们团队迄今为止所取得的成就感到自豪。

我认为，一旦某种类型的人尝到了在初创公司工作的滋味，他们就永远无法想象回到大公司了。 你不能把老虎放回笼子里。 确切地。 很难回去。

作为一名企业家，您在此过程中学到了什么，您希望一开始就知道吗？

老实说，我知道这将是很多长时间和肘部油脂。 从第一天开始就是这样，这也是它如此令人兴奋的原因，也是我想要全职工作的原因。 它只是一根持续不断的消防水带，本质上是一块空白的画布，心中有一个巨大的最终目标——创造很酷的东西并发展公司。 随之而来的自由是无与伦比的。

但我也知道，如果我们提前付出努力，最终事情会变得容易一些，现在我们拥有一支由非常敏锐、可靠的人组成的了不起的团队，每个人都带来了很多东西。 现在回顾我们所做和建造的一切，我想如果我一开始就知道过去几年的努力程度，那将是完全可怕的。 所以我很高兴我不知道这一切。 但我很高兴它展现了它迄今为止的表现。

这很有趣，因为我总是希望有人给我这个神奇的建议，而最好的办法就是不知道，因为它可能会吓跑你。

当我准备这次采访时，我正在查看你的社交媒体，我真的很好奇anger.unicorns.lol是什么，因为它看起来与你所做的其他事情非常不同。

它们是我的创作。 我几年前开始画画，我无法在纸上画画来挽救我的生命，但在 Illustrator 中制作它们真的很有趣。 其中很多都被做成了贴纸，人们不断地索取。 所以我会把包裹带到我的旧办公室，然后把它们扔到桌子上供人们拿，然后我把它们邮寄到世界各地几次。 其中一些对我来说真的很特别，是从我生命中的特殊时刻诞生的。 但最终，它们只是一种发泄方式，如果其他人喜欢它，那我就会很开心。 所以这只是一件有趣的事情。

什么是 OpenSOC.io？

这实际上是埃里克多年前的创意。 当他还在德克萨斯州公共安全部时，他就开始为他的安全团队运行它。 他想把它变成更令人兴奋的东西，所以它变成了 OpenSOC，我们建立了它。 这是一个蓝队 CTF。 我们基于开源和“每个人都可以访问”的工具构建了比赛。 我们经营自己的范围。 这是一个完整的企业环境，包含所有的花里胡哨。 我们有用户自动化、流量生成和邮件生成。 它会生成大量遥测数据，并将所有这些数据发送到我们的 OpenSOC 安全堆栈。 我们为参与者提供一套开源 DFIR 工具，例如OpenSearch或最可能知道的ELK 、 Velociraptor 、 osquery 、 Arkime （以前称为 Moloch），然后我们基本上执行一个或多个场景，就好像我们是各种威胁一样演员或 APT。 所以参与者然后成为事件响应者，他们必须去寻找整个环境中的所有工件，并基本上将事件拼凑起来。

所以当他们在那里时，他们可以访问防火墙和 IDS 日志、Thinkst Canary 日志、Windows 和 Linux 系统日志、数据包捕获。 他们有能力使用 osquery 和 Velociraptor 询问所有端点。 所以，所有的事情。 然后他们开始寻找威胁。 他们试图弄清楚攻击者是否泄露了任何数据？ 有什么影响？ 他们是如何进入的，他们访问了什么？ 是否有任何横向移动，哪些系统受到影响，甚至，如何防止这种情况发生？ 等等。

我们已经运行它几年了。 我们已经在全国范围内为成千上万的人举办了很多会议，包括DEF CON多年。 我们是最大的蓝队 CTF，并在那里获得了Black Badge的地位。 我们已经在一系列 BSides 活动、 CactusCon 、 DakotaCon上运行它。 德州网络峰会是另一个不错的峰会，还有其他一些峰会。 我们还运行我们的 NDR 培训平台，该平台本质上是从 OpenSOC 发展而来的，我们每季度通过 Recon 提供它。 这就是我们今年在Black Hat上再次运行的那个。 如您所知，我们已将 LimaCharlie 集成到产品中，这令人兴奋。

所以我们对此很兴奋，但这绝对是一种爱的劳动。 这对业内很多人来说真的很有价值，而且很有趣

我们对此感到非常兴奋。 我知道 Maxime 实际上会去那里，因为他想在那里回答问题，但这听起来像是你制作的一款非常棒的软件。

是的，我听说前几天他要加入我们。 这很令人兴奋，而且会很有趣。

这个不在左侧字段中，但是制表符还是空格？

我觉得我应该用硅谷愤怒的理查德 gif 来回应。 总是制表符。 今天早上我还在和它打架。

我自己就是一个标签人。 我认为Maxime type: embedded-entry-inline id: 4QZSGFJ4hIO7P03UY8nmtZ _可能是有点尴尬的空格。 我觉得这很合适。 他似乎有这个想法。

是的。 不过我们有一个 linter，所以我不必考虑太多。

你去吧。

当你接受一个新客户或进入一个新组织时，你是否经常看到一些可以被认为是唾手可得的事情？ 作为他们可以做的最简单的事情来改善他们的安全状况，您会对一般组织说些什么？

是的，绝对的。 有时只是确保他们尽可能获得 MFA。 它出现的频率仍然让我感到惊讶。 单点登录也是一样。 我们仍然可以在野外看到 Windows 7 和 2003。 我们必须摆脱那些小家伙。 它仍然时不时地让你感到惊讶。 利用端点保护，无论是 Defender 还是其他供应商。 当我们加入某人时，我们通常会尽快在所有端点上获得代理，以确保我们尽快获得遥测。 不过，我认为最大的一项就是安全意识培训。 我知道我们都讨厌坐着看那些超级有趣的视频，而且我们不得不这样做多年，但它确实有好处。 有些人根本不知道，然后他们被击中，什么时候可以防止它。 尤其是网络钓鱼培训。 我们行业的人仍然受到它的打击，我们应该更了解。 所以我觉得即使是这么简单的事情最终也会变得非常巨大。

您对当前的网络安全供应商市场有何看法？ 网络安全因拥有太多流行语之类的东西而臭名昭著。 是的，我们总是拿那些烦人的流行语开玩笑。 但我觉得多年来，整个行业一直由拥有巨额营销预算的大公司和闪亮的大公司主导。 看到一些小公司在这个领域真正有技术、有能力和有效地做大事，这真是令人耳目一新。 真是太棒了。

我同意这种情绪。

我相信你会的。

是否有公司或思想领袖或社区成员被您视为在网络安全中做正确事情的人的好榜样？

除了 Recon 之外，我最喜欢看的一些公司和领导者： Andrew Morris和GreyNoise 、 Haroon Meer和Thinkst Canary 、 Mike Cohen和 Velociraptor。 我们与 Mike 合作了很长时间，他的平台非常棒。 你们和 LimaCharlie 显然，仅举几例。 我认为这些是超级敏锐团队构建令人难以置信的平台的很好的例子，他们显然已经投入了很多，然后他们仍然回馈社区。 这一直是我们在 Recon 的坚实核心价值——始终回馈社区。 这就是我提到的那些公司从一开始就一直在做的事情，而且做得非常好。 我觉得这很牛逼。 安全是很难的，当有一个巨大的美元符号进入障碍时就更难了，我觉得那些人做得对，让进入那里更容易一点。

是的，我认为总体上每个人都受益于采取我们共同建立这种安全态势的态度。 我真的很讨厌所有这一切的商业方面的零和思维。 现在的问题比解决问题的人还多。 所以让我们不要担心谁得到什么，让我们真诚地前进，我们都会一起到达那里。

对于那些在职业生涯早期考虑进入网络安全领域的人，您有什么建议吗？

是的，深入研究。安全性的传播范围广泛而深入，有很多途径需要考虑。 更不用说安全行业的工作保障了。 我们将永远需要它，更重要的是，它很有趣，也很有挑战性。 就像我之前说的，它是一根消防水带，总有新的东西要学习和成长的空间，而且永远不会无聊。 自满不是一种选择。 所以我觉得如果你喜欢挑战，这是一个很好的时间和一个好地方。

你对未来有什么预测吗？

考虑到这个国家的状况，基本上是现在的世界，这感觉像是一个沉重的问题。 想想就有点害怕。 但我会说我看到 Recon 在成长。 我们现在正在做大事，这对我们的团队来说是一个非常激动人心的时刻。 所以我很期待看到我们接下来的发展方向，但我也预测在两周内与 LimaCharlie 一起在 Black Hat 上取得巨大成功。

哇，它来得很快，不是吗？

没错，就在拐角处。 所以这会很有趣。



原文:

Please introduce yourself and tell us what you do.

My name is Whitney Champion and I'm the lead architect and one of the co-founders of Recon InfoSec. Basically I'm responsible for building and maintaining our security stack, our applications, and also our training platform: The Network Defense Range, or NDR. We're a managed security services provider, and we're based out of Austin, Texas. There are roughly 15 of us and we provide managed detection and response services and training.

Very cool. What made you want to start an MDR Company?

Well Eric (Capuano) had actually been running Recon solo for a couple years before I joined him. It was kind of a side gig for him. He's been in the security industry for a long time too. Initially I joined him in his efforts to get one of our side projects, OpenSOC, up and running. I joined him to help get that automated and bring it to life because he'd been doing it on his own and I had cycles to jump in. So after we worked together on that for a little while, I started doing work for Recon on the side. I was still full-time at my day job, working with Red Hat, and I really missed the startup life. Before I was at Red Hat, I was at a company that had a head count of about 40 people when I started, and then we grew to about 400. I jumped over to Red Hat and that’s, as you know, a pretty big company. I really missed being in the small environment, I missed the grind, and building things from the ground up.

I loved what we were doing at Recon and knew we could make an impact in the industry, and so did Eric. We needed full-time infrastructure work, and security engineering. So, eventually I went full time for Recon when we were able to, and a few years later here we are.

It's been a wild ride, but I'm really proud of what our team has accomplished so far.

_That's great. I think once a certain type of person gets a taste of what it is like to work in a startup they can never imagine going back to a big company. You can't put the tiger back in the cage. _

Exactly. It's hard to go back.

Is there anything you've learned along the way as an entrepreneur that you wish you had known at the beginning?

To be honest, I knew it would be a lot of long hours and elbow grease. It was that way since day one, and it's kind of what made it so exciting and what made me want to go full-time. It was just a constant fire hose, and it was essentially a blank canvas with a huge end goal in mind–to build cool things and grow the company. The freedom that comes with that is unmatched.

But I also knew that if we put in the hard work up front, eventually things would get a little easier, and now we have an amazing team of really sharp, reliable people, and everybody brings a lot to the table. Looking back now at everything we've done and built, I think if I had known in the beginning the level of effort that would go into the last few years, it would have been completely terrifying. So I am glad I didn't know the whole of it. But I'm very thrilled that it unfolded how it has so far.

That’s funny because I always want somebody to give me this piece of magic advice when the best thing is to not know because it would probably scare you off.

As I was preparing for this interview, I was checking out your social media and I'm really curious what angry.unicorns.lol is, because it seems very different from the rest of the things you do.

They're my creations. I started drawing years ago, and I can't draw on paper to save my life, but making them in Illustrator was really fun. A lot of them were made into stickers and people kept asking for them. So I would bring bundles into my old office and throw them out on the table for people to take, and I mailed them around the globe a few times. And some of them are really special to me and were birthed from special points in my life. But ultimately, they were just kind of an outlet and if other people enjoy it then that makes me happy. So it's just a fun thing to do.

_I totally get it, you need to have that creative energy go somewhere. _

What is OpenSOC.io?

It's actually Eric's brainchild from years ago. He started running it for his security team when he was still at the Texas Department of Public Safety. He wanted to turn it into something more exciting, so it became OpenSOC, and we built it. It's a BlueTeam CTF. We built the competition based on open source and ‘accessible to everybody’ tools. We run our own range. It's a full enterprise environment with all the bells and whistles. We’ve got user automation, traffic generation, and mail generation. It generates a ton of telemetry and all that gets shipped to our OpenSOC security stack. We provide participants with a suite of open source DFIR tools like OpenSearch, or ELK as most probably know it, Velociraptor, osquery, Arkime (which was formerly known as Moloch) and then we essentially execute one or more scenarios as if we were various threat actors or APTs. So the participants then become the incident responders, and they have to go find all the artifacts throughout the environment and basically piece together the incident.

So when they're in there, they've got access to firewall and IDS logs, Thinkst Canary logs, Windows and Linux system logs, packet captures. They have the ability to interrogate all the endpoints with osquery and Velociraptor. So, all the things. And then they start threat hunting. They’re trying to figure out things like, did the attacker exfil any data? What is the impact? How did they get in, and what did they access? Was there any lateral movement, what systems were affected, or even, how could this have been prevented? And so on.

We've been running it for a handful of years now. We've run it around the country for thousands of people at a ton of conferences, including DEF CON for several years. We were the largest blue team CTF and achieved Black Badge status there. We've run it at a bunch of BSides events, CactusCon, DakotaCon. Texas Cyber Summit was another good one, and some others. We also run our NDR training platform, which essentially grew out of OpenSOC, and we offer it quarterly through Recon. That's the one that we're running again at Black Hat this year. And as you know we've integrated LimaCharlie into the offering, which is exciting.

So we're stoked about that, but it's definitely a labor of love. It's been really valuable to a lot of people in the industry, and it’s been fun

We're super excited about it. I know Maxime is actually going to go down there because he wants to be there to answer questions, but it sounds like a really amazing piece of software you’ve made.

Yeah, I heard that he was going to join us the other day. That's exciting, and will be fun.

This one's out of left field, but tabs or spaces?

I feel like I should respond with an angry Richard gif from Silicon Valley. Always tabs. I was fighting with it just this morning.

I'm a tab person myself. I think Maxime type: embedded-entry-inline id: 4QZSGFJ4hIO7P03UY8nmtZ _might be spaces which is a little awkward. _

I feel like that's fitting. He seems like he's got the mind for it.

Yeah. We have a linter though, so I don't have to think about it too much.

There you go.

When you take on a new client or go into a new organization is there something that you often see that could be considered low-hanging fruit? What would you say to organizations in general as the simplest thing they can do to improve their security posture?

Yeah, absolutely. Sometimes it's simply making sure they've got MFA wherever possible. It still kind of surprises me how frequently that comes up. Same thing with single sign on. We still see Windows 7 and 2003 in the wild. We've got to get rid of those little guys. It still takes you by surprise every now and then. Take advantage of endpoint protection, whether it's Defender or some other vendor. We typically get agents on all of our endpoints as soon as we can when we onboard someone just to ensure that we're getting telemetry as soon as possible. I think one of the biggest ones, though, is simply security awareness training. I know we all hate sitting through it and watching those super fun videos, and we’ve had to do it for years now, but it has real benefits. Some people simply don't know and then they get hit, when it could have been prevented. Especially phishing training. People in our industry still get hit by it and we’re the ones that are supposed to know better. So I feel like even something as simple as that can end up being pretty huge.

What are your thoughts on the current cybersecurity vendor market? Cybersecurity is infamous for having too many buzzwords and things like that. Yeah, we joke about the annoying buzzwords all the time. But I feel like for years, this whole industry has been dominated by the big companies with huge marketing budgets and the big shiny whatever. It's really refreshing to see some smaller companies in the limelight that are really technical, capable, and effective doing big things in this space. It's kind of awesome.

I share that sentiment.

I'm sure you do.

Are there companies or thought leaders or community members that you look to as good examples of people that are doing the right kinds of things in cybersecurity?

Some of my favorite companies and leaders to watch, besides Recon obviously: Andrew Morris and GreyNoise, Haroon Meer and Thinkst Canary, Mike Cohen and Velociraptor. We've been working with Mike for a long time and his platform is amazing. You guys and LimaCharlie obviously, to name a few. Fleet is another awesome company, and awesome team. I think those are really good examples of super sharp teams building incredible platforms that they've clearly poured so much into, and then they still give back to the community. That has always been a solid core value of ours at Recon–to always give back to the community. It's what those companies I mentioned have always done from the get go, and in really big ways. I think that's pretty badass. Security is hard and it's even harder when there's a huge dollar sign barrier to entry, and I feel like those folks are doing it right by making it a little bit easier to get in there.

Yeah, I think overall everybody benefits by taking the attitude that we go together and build this security posture together. I really hate the zero sum thinking on the business side of all of this. There are more problems than there are people to solve them right now. So let's not worry about who gets what piece and let's just just go forward in good faith that we're all gonna get there together.

Do you have any advice for people early in their careers that are considering getting into cybersecurity?

Yes, dive in. Security spreads far and wide and deep and there are so many avenues to consider. Not to mention there's job security in the security industry. We will always need it, and more importantly, it's fun and it's challenging. Like I said earlier, it is a fire hose and there's always something new to learn and room to grow, and it’s never boring. Complacency is not an option. So I feel like if you like a challenge, it's a pretty good time and a good place to be.

Do you have any predictions for the future?

This feels like a loaded question given the state of the country, and basically the world right now. It's a little scary to think about. But I will say that I see Recon growing. We're building big things right now, and it's a really exciting time for our team. So I'm looking forward to seeing where we go next, but I also predict a smashing success at Black Hat with LimaCharlie in two weeks.

Wow, it's coming up quick, isn't it?

Right, right around the corner. So that will be fun.
item.author: charltonlc

item.title: “我们有问题......”👨‍🚀（python com windows） | "We have a problem..."👨‍🚀(python com windows)
item.description:
娜塔莉亚·卡通达

发表于瞧！

一旦暂停，nataliacatundafr 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，nataliacatundafr 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，由 nataliacatundafr 发布的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 nataliacatundafr 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 nataliacatundafr 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 nataliacatundafr 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Natália Catunda

Posted on Aug 19

Esses comandos servem para você salvar tudo vez em que sentir aquela dificuldadezinha em instalar o python no seu windows 10.

  1. Instalando o Chocolatey: Vá até o Power Shell como administrador(botão direito do mouse). 
Set-ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Scope Process -Force; [System.Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [System.Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol -bor 3072; iex ((New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('https://community.chocolatey.org/install.ps1'))
  1. instalando o python com chocolatey: Vá até o cmd (prompt de comando) 
choco install python3 --pre

Agora é só reiniciar a janela do seu projeto! e voilá!

Once suspended, nataliacatundafr will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, nataliacatundafr will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by nataliacatundafr will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If nataliacatundafr is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag nataliacatundafr:

nataliacatundafr consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging nataliacatundafr will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: nataliacatundafr

item.title: 数据工程 101：数据工程简介 | Data Engineering 101: Introduction to Data Engineering
item.description:
约翰·曼波

发表于随着全球技术的飞速发展，数据处理已成为一项巨大挑战，尤其是当数据必须从存储转移到用户，从不同的原始格式转移到新格式而不失去其价值时。
为了处理这些数据，数据工程是必不可少的。

什么是数据工程？

数据工程是构建/架构数据平台、设计和实施数据存储和存储库、数据湖以及收集、导入、清理、预处理、查询、分析数据、性能监控、评估、优化和微调的艺术流程和系统。 它使数据可用于分析和有效的数据驱动决策。

数据工程师的角色是什么？

数据工程师的职责是存储、提取、转换、加载、聚合和验证数据。
这涉及：

  • 为需要查询数据的工具构建数据管道并有效地存储数据。

  • 分析数据，确保其遵守数据治理规则和法规。

  • 了解数据存储和查询选项的优缺点。

数据工程师提供：

  • 正确的数据。

  • 以正确的形式。

  • 给正确的人。

  • 尽可能高效。

数据工程师负责：

  • 从不同来源摄取数据。

  • 优化用于分析的数据库。

  • 删除损坏的数据。

  • 开发、构建、测试和维护数据架构。

为什么数据工程很重要？

数据工程生命周期包括构建数据平台、设计和实施数据存储、存储库和数据湖，以及收集、传递、清理、预处理、查询、分析数据、性能监控、评估、优化和调整系统。
各种规模的公司都有大量不同的数据需要梳理，以回答关键的业务问题。 数据工程旨在支持该过程，使数据消费者（例如分析师、数据科学家和高管）能够可靠、快速、安全地检查所有可用数据。

数据工程工具和技能

数据工程师使用许多工具来处理数据。 他们使用一套专门的技能来创建端到端的数据管道，将数据从源系统移动到目标目的地。
数据工程师使用各种工具和技术，例如：

  • 他们访问数据，然后应用规则通过步骤“转换”数据，使其更适合分析。

  • 云数据存储：包括Amazon S3、Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS)、谷歌云存储等。

  • 查询引擎：引擎对数据运行查询以返回答案。 数据工程师可能会使用 Dremio Sonar、Spark、Flink 等引擎。

  • 数据工程师可以选择将 Python 用于 ETL 任务。

  • 数据工程与数据科学

原文:

john mambo

Posted on Aug 19

With the tremendous growth of technology throughout the World, Data handling has been a big challenge especially when the data has to be moved from storage to users, from different original formats to new formats without losing its value.
To handle this data, Data Engineering is essential.

What is Data Engineering?

Data Engineering is the art of building/architecting data platforms, designing and implementing data stores and repositories, data lakes and gathering, importing, cleaning, pre-processing, querying, analyzing data, performance monitoring, evaluation, optimization, and fine-tuning the processes and systems. It makes Data available for analysis and efficient Data-driven decision-making.

What is the Role of a Data Engineer?

It’s the role of a data engineer to store, extract, transform, load, aggregate, and validate data.
This involves:

  • Building data pipelines and efficiently storing data for tools that need to query the data.

  • Analyzing the data, ensuring it adheres to data governance rules and regulations.

  • Understanding the pros and cons of data storage and query options.

Data Engineers deliver:

  • The Correct data.

  • In the Correct Form.

  • To the Right People.

  • As Efficiently as Possible.

Data Engineers are Responsible for:

  • Ingesting Data from different Sources.

  • Optimizing Databases for Analysis.

  • Removing Corrupted Data.

  • Develop, Construct, test, and Maintain Data Architecture.

Why is Data Engineering important?

The Data Engineering lifecycle consists of building data platforms, designing and implementing Data Stores, Repos, and Data Lakes, and gathering, imparting, cleaning, preprocessing, querying, analyzing data, performance monitoring, evaluation, optimization, and tuning the System.
Companies of all sizes have huge amounts of disparate data to comb through to answer critical business questions. Data engineering is designed to support the process, making it possible for consumers of data, such as analysts, data scientists, and executives, to reliably, quickly, and securely inspect all of the data available.

Data Engineering Tools and skills

Data Engineers use many tools to work with Data. They use a specialized skill set to create end-to-end data pipelines that move Data from Source systems to target destinations.
Data Engineers work with a variety of tools and technologies, such as:

  • ETL Tools: ETL (extract, transform, load) tools move data between systems. They access data, then apply rules to “transform” the data through steps that make it more suitable for analysis.

  • Cloud Data Storage: Including Amazon S3, Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS), Google Cloud Storage, etc.

  • Query Engines: Engines run queries against data to return answers. Data engineers may work with engines like Dremio Sonar, Spark, Flink, and others.

  • Python: Python is a general programming language. Data engineers may choose to use Python for ETL tasks.

  • SQL: Structured Query Language (SQL) is the standard language for querying relational databases.

Data Engineering Versus Data Science

item.title: 关于技术的 8 个惊人而有趣的事实可能会让你大吃一惊 | 8 Amazing and Interesting Facts About Technology That Might Surprise You
item.description:
世界正变得越来越技术先进，但人们也越来越难以跟上最新的创新。 人们对每天的技术变化感到不知所措并不少见，但我们必须保持关注，以免落后。

当前正在改变我们生活的发展技术有哪些？ 技术的未来总是在变化。 人工智能和机器人领域的新发展正在改变我们的生活、工作和互动方式。

目前正在改变我们生活的一些发展技术是：

这些设备通常具有传感器和其他组件，以提供有关个人物理环境的信息，例如高度、声级、振动水平、磁北或真北方向等。

它通常是通过将视频图像投影到用户面前的半透明屏幕上来实现的，用户戴着 3D 眼镜可以看到完整的立体图像

以下是该技术带来的一些变化的简要列表：

人工智能 (AI) 是计算机科学的一个分支，它处理计算机中智能行为的模拟。 人工智能已成为现代世界的重要组成部分。 它现在被广泛应用于工程、医学、科学、法律等多个领域。

人工智能几乎被应用于每个行业，它对未来消费主义的影响只是众多行业之一。 借助人工智能，消费者可以获得更加个性化的产品，以满足他们的需求和需求。

本节将介绍为什么人工智能是消费主义的未来，它将如何改变我们的消费方式，它将如何改变营销人员销售产品的方式，以及为什么人工智能为消费者提供更多机会。

该领域的成立是基于这样一种说法，即有可能生产出能够执行人类完成需要智能的任务的机器。 半个多世纪以来，人工智能一直是学术界内外深入研究的主题。 它是计算机科学、数学、心理学、生物学、经济学、逻辑学和哲学领域的专家们探索和研究的领域。

手机是自 2000 年代初到近代以来最常见的发明之一。 它们非常受欢迎，你经常会发现没有它的人不能长时间、一天或几个小时。

它们有多种用途，已成为我们日常生活的重要组成部分。

想象一个带有 SIM 卡的微型设备，它可以在世界任何地方拨打语音电话、发送短信和访问互联网。 仅此一项就令人叹为观止。

普通人每天查看手机 150 次。 结果，手机的电池寿命不断消耗，如果您不知道如何正确为手机充电，可能会导致不必要的压力。 您应该仅在电池电量不足且电量不足一半时为手机充电，以便充分利用您的设备。

搜索引擎是用户获取信息的主要来源。 他们通过在互联网上搜索来帮助他们找到所需的信息。 实际上，与以前相比，您今天花费在搜索上的时间几乎没有。 这对我们如何在现代世界中节省时间产生了巨大影响。

市场上有两个主要参与者，谷歌和必应。 雅虎一直在慢慢失去对市场的控制，并逐渐淡出人们的视线。

互联网是世界上最强大和最广泛的计算机网络系统。 互联网是美国国防部于 1973 年发明的。 它最初被称为 ARPANET，它的创建是为了允许计算机之间进行长距离通信。

第一个网站现在被称为“互联网简史”，由 Tim Berners-Lee 于 1991 年推出，他自 1980 年以来一直致力于一个名为 INQUIRE 的项目。

互联网自发明以来呈指数级增长，现在它在全球拥有超过 30 亿用户，在任何特定时刻平均有 60 亿个活跃连接。

域名注册是创建网络形象的第一步。 这是任何企业或个人建立在线数字足迹的有效方式。

来源： 我的博客



原文:

The world is becoming more and more technologically advanced, but it’s also becoming more and more difficult for people to keep up with the latest innovations. It’s not uncommon for people to feel overwhelmed by the changes in technology from day to day, but it’s imperative that we stay tuned in so that we don’t fall behind.

What are the current developmental technologies that are changing our lives? (wikipedia pages are not included)
The future of technology is always changing. New developments in the field of AI and robotics are changing the way we live, work, and interact with one another.

Some current developmental technologies that are changing our lives are:

– Wearable technology: It is the use of small electronic devices that can be worn on clothing or carried in a pocket or handbag. These devices typically have sensors and other components to provide information about one’s physical environment, such as altitude, sound level, vibration levels, orientation to magnetic north or true north, etc.

– Augmented Reality: It is a live direct or indirect view of a physical real-world environment whose elements are augmented by computer-generated sensory input such as sound waves; it is typically achieved by projecting a video image onto a translucent screen in front of the user, who wears 3D glasses to see the full stereoscopic image

Here is a brief list of some of the changes that the technology has brought:

1) Why AI is the future (technology facts)

Artificial intelligence (AI) is a branch of computer science that deals with the simulation of intelligent behavior in computers. AI has become an essential part of the modern world. It is now being used extensively in a number of fields such as engineering, medicine, science, law and other areas.

AI is being applied to almost every industry and its impact on the future of consumerism is just one of the many. With AI, consumers can get more personalized products that are tailored to their needs and wants.

This section will cover why AI is the future of consumerism, how it will change how we consume, how it will change how marketers sell their products and why AI provides more opportunities for consumers.

The term “artificial intelligence” was coined by John McCarthy in 1955 at Dartmouth College. The field was founded on the claim that it is possible to produce machines capable of performing tasks that would require intelligence if done by humans. Artificial intelligence has been the subject of intense study for over half a century both within and outside academia. It is a field that has been explored and researched by experts in computer science, mathematics, psychology, biology, economics, logic, and philosophy.

2) The mobile devices (cell phones)

Mobile phones are one of the most common inventions since early 2000s to recent history. They are very popular and you will often find people who can’t go a long time, day or maybe a few hours without it.

They are used for a variety of purposes and they have become an essential part of our daily lives.

Imagine a tiny device with a SIM card that can make voice calls, send text messages and access the internet – anywhere in the world. That alone is breathtaking.

The average person checks their phone 150 times per day. As a result, the battery life of your phone is constantly draining and if you don’t know how to charge your phone properly, it can lead to unnecessary stress. You should only charge your phone when the battery is low and half drained so that you can get the most out of your device.

3) The search engine

The search engines are a major source of information for the users. They help them find the information they need by searching for it on the internet. Actually, the amount of time you spend searching today is barely anything compared to before. And that has a huge impact on how we save time in modern world.

There are two major players in the market, Google and Bing. Yahoo has been slowly losing its grip on the market and is gradually fading into obscurity.

4) WWW / world wide web (world’s first webpage and world of technology now)

The Internet is the world’s most powerful and widespread system of computer networks. The Internet was invented in 1973 by the U.S Department of Defense. It was originally called ARPANET and it was created to allow computers to communicate with each other over long distances.

The first website, which is now known as “A Brief History of the Internet,” was launched in 1991, by Tim Berners-Lee, who had been working on a project called ENQUIRE since 1980.

The internet has grown exponentially since its invention and now it has more than 3 billion users worldwide with an average of 6 billion active connections at any given moment.

A domain name registration is the first step to creating your web presence. Which is an effective way for any business or individuals to establish an online digital footprint.

The source: my blog

Compare number using type only

export type CreateArrayWithLengthX< LENGTH extends number, ACC extends unknown[] = []
> = ACC['length'] extends LENGTH ? ACC : CreateArrayWithLengthX<LENGTH, [...ACC, 1]> export type CompareAToB< A extends number[], B extends number[]
> = A['length'] extends B['length'] ? 'equal' : A extends [infer C, ...infer D extends number[]] ? B extends [infer M, ...infer N extends number[]] ? CompareAToB<D, N> : 'greater' : B['length'] extends 0 ? 'equal' : 'lesser' type E = CompareAToB<CreateArrayWithLengthX<99>,CreateArrayWithLengthX<100>> // lesser
// ^?
type F = CompareAToB<CreateArrayWithLengthX<100>,CreateArrayWithLengthX<100>> // equal 
// ^?
type G = CompareAToB<CreateArrayWithLengthX<101>,CreateArrayWithLengthX<100>> // greater
